title: Explain Display Actions
short_answer: Display actions run routed screen commands.
text: display_actions reads routed events from runtime/queues/router_dispatch/action_queue.jsonl when target_runner is display_actions. The service code is in app/display_actions/runners/display_action_router.py and it writes results, screenshots, and status under runtime/display_actions.

title: Explain Display Action Queue
short_answer: The queue feeds work to display_actions.
text: Display action requests come from runtime/queues/router_dispatch/action_queue.jsonl and must include target_runner set to display_actions. display_action_router.py skips other events and remembers the last processed line in display_action_runner_status.json.

title: Explain Screenshot Capture
short_answer: Screenshot capture saves a PNG of the screen.
text: take_screenshot and take_full_screenshot are handled by app/display_actions/runners/screenshot_capture_runner.py. PNG files are saved in runtime/display_actions/screenshots, with standard captures cropped/resized and full captures using the full monitor mode.

title: Explain Screen Analysis
short_answer: Screen analysis asks the vision model about a screenshot.
text: analyze_screen and analyze_last_screenshot run through app/display_actions/runners/screenshot_analyze_runner.py and app/llm/vision_service.py. analyze_screen can reuse a recent screenshot, while analyze_last_screenshot fails with no_previous_screenshot if runtime/display_actions/screenshots has no PNG.

title: Explain Display Action Results
short_answer: Results and status show what display_actions did.
text: Display action results are appended to runtime/display_actions/results/display_action_results.jsonl with ok, action_name, speech_text, and metadata. Service health is in runtime/display_actions/status/display_action_runner_status.json and is also shown by the local library Display Actions area.
